---
title: "ferx"
subtitle: "Fast nonlinear mixed effects modeling with a Rust backend"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

ferx provides FOCE/FOCEI estimation with exact gradients via the Enzyme autodiff toolchain.
It supports analytical PK models (1- and 2-compartment) and ODE-based models, with a clean R API backed by a high-performance Rust engine ([ferx-core](https://github.com/FeRx-NLME/ferx-core)).

## Install

```r
# Requires Rust (cargo) — see Get started for full setup
remotes::install_github("FeRx-NLME/ferx-r")
```

## Quick example

```r
library(ferx)
paths <- ferx_example("warfarin")
fit   <- ferx_fit(paths$model, paths$data)
print(fit)
```

---

[Get started →](get-started.qmd)
